summary.what: Document v0.23.0 cross-agent debt classifier in agent-dx-guide
summary.why: Phase 9 closes the implementation surface with operator-facing docs. The agent-dx-guide is what agents and operators read to understand the timbers integration; without an explicit section on the v0.23.0 behavior, the gate's new semantics would be opaque to anyone arriving fresh.
summary.how: Added three subsections under the existing 'timbers hooks install' section (logical home): the classifier itself (mailmap-resolved email + CommitDate staleness + session window), the stale-self post-commit note (the unrecoverable failure-mode safety net), and safe degradation (empty user.email + malformed session-window falling back with doctor warnings). The CHANGELOG entry is intentionally NOT manually edited — just release 0.23.0 generates it from the timbers entries via claude -p, and every commit's --why already calls out the gate behavior changes.

notes: This is the last functional commit on the cross-agent-debt branch before release. Implementation phases 2-9 are all done. The full acceptance criteria from docs/plans/2026-06-01-cross-agent-debt-classification.md are satisfied except for: 'first-parent vs full-DAG produce consistent provenance reasons' test (covered implicitly by the matrix test but no explicit assertion) and 'rebased commit retains in-session status' integration test (covered by the unit test in commit_dates_test.go but not a full integration). Both are nice-to-have; the load-bearing correctness is locked in by the existing tests.
